title: The Wandering of Giordano Bruno
subtitle: A heretic's road across Europe, 1548–1600
subject: giordano-bruno
design_note: Alchemical Noir: the original for this template — a single luminous route across a darkened Europe, ending where the reader who plays it through already fears. Evidence lives in the line: solid attested, dashed reconstructed.
provenance_note: Itinerary after standard biographies (Frances Yates, Giordano Bruno and the Hermetic Tradition; Ingrid Rowland, Giordano Bruno: Philosopher/Heretic; Hilary Gatti). Datings are approximate where marked. Travel legs marked 'inferred' have no direct documentary record and reconstruct the most likely route between attested locations.

stops.what: Born Filippo Bruno in the shadow of Vesuvius; the small town whose name he carried as 'the Nolan'.
Studies humanities and dialectic; exposed to the memory arts and Lullism that shape his whole project.
Enters the Dominican order at San Domenico Maggiore; takes the name Giordano. Doubts on the Trinity bring an indictment for heresy — he flees, discarding his habit.
After three obscure years wandering northern Italy, surfaces in Calvinist Geneva; quickly clashes with the authorities and moves on.
Lectures on Aristotle and the sphere; earns a doctorate and a teaching post.
Patronage of Henri III. Publishes De umbris idearum on the art of memory; his reputation as a magus is made.
Lodges at the French embassy. Publishes the great Italian dialogues — La cena de le ceneri, De l'infinito universo e mondi — defending an infinite, Copernican cosmos.
Lectures on the immortality of the soul and Copernicus; the dons rebuff him. A brief, bruising excursion.
stops.source: Cena de le ceneri (his own account, partisan)

stops.what: Teaches at the Lutheran university; a rare settled, productive interval. Delivers a famous valedictory oration.
Briefly at the court of Rudolf II; dedicates a work on mathematics and magic to the emperor.
At the great book fair; sees the Latin poems (De triplici minimo, De monade) through the press — his mature metaphysics of the minimum.
Lured back to Italy by the patrician Giovanni Mocenigo, ostensibly to teach the art of memory. Mocenigo denounces him to the Inquisition.
Extradited to Rome. Seven years imprisoned and interrogated; refuses to recant. On 17 February 1600 he is burned at the Campo de' Fiori, a nail driven through his tongue.
stops.source: Roman trial summary; Firpo, Il processo di Giordano Bruno
